items.name: Interleukin 33
items.summary: IL33 encodes interleukin-33, an epithelial-derived cytokine often described as an 'alarmin' that can be released during barrier stress or injury. IL-33 can promote type 2 immune responses via signaling through the IL1RL1 (ST2) receptor on immune cells such as ILC2s and mast cells. Population genetic studies repeatedly implicate the IL33/IL1RL1 region in asthma and allergic phenotypes, motivating mechanistic hypotheses that connect airway exposures to epithelial stress signaling and downstream inflammation.
